artifacts.title: Consciousness receipt from 2025-02-18
artifacts.excerpt:
I once talked to someone at a party in college that made one brilliant point after another at the intersection of philosophy of mind and economics.

He was crystal clear in his thinking and he had an elaborate (but not overfit as far as I can tell) model of how society was not made of individuals, but rather of attention streams. Rather than carving the social ontology at the individual, he would break it down in terms of attention types and degrees of signal to noise ratio in verbal actions. He viewed economic activity in terms of attention streams recruiting each other for information processing and resource extraction. And so on. He said he was putting this into writing and emailing professors about it, and I wished him luck. He apparently got into a fight with someone at the group house hosting the party later that night but I never learned what exactly happened.

About a week later

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2026-01-01

If you're young and intellectually gifted, IMO you should *still* try to get into Harvard, Yale, MIT, Stanford, Oxford, Cambridge, etc.

Just because these institutions are indeed on a path of long-term decline shouldn't be a reason to underestimate their current relevance or their significance for the foreseeable future!

Please don't self-sabotage to feel cool or 'above it'. They're still force-multipliers.

artifacts.title: Which receipt from 2025-11-07
artifacts.excerpt:
I met someone at a party who was very into biohacking and was explaining his stack to a group of people.

He showed us his travel case, which contained 80 different pill bottles, which he takes every day. Everything finely tuned to subtly influence his brain and body for nuanced healthy beneficial effects. From mild stimulants like Rhodiola Rosea and Cola, to relaxing agents like l-theanine and magnolia bark extract, to herbal nootropics like bacopa and alpha GPC, you know, the usual. He takes pills three times a day, carefully timed to his life's activities and needs.

"For writing or concentration?" I asked him. He said "lots of caffeine". And then also opened his shirt - revealing a nicotine patch. "What I like to do is overshoot the caffeine and nicotine a bit, and then temper it with some GHB". I thought "Oh wow first time I hear someone using GHB as nootropic, sick!".

Then people
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: which, these, other, way.

artifacts.title: Experience receipt from 2025-11-10
artifacts.excerpt: Omg! This actually does happen at the Indra’s Net, high-dose DMT level! You see the endless copies of faces? Sometimes you’ll hallucinate your own sense of self getting endlessly replicated in a feedback loop like this. The crazy part is that as the copies become increasingly compressed and pressurized, aliasing effects start to appear, and when you hit the Nyquist frequency, the gestalt changes texture. That’s when you realize that every part of the experience was, and always had been, the entirety of the experience, just projected and contorted so it looks like something else from the strange angle you’re viewing it from.

artifacts.title: Experience receipt from 2025-05-07
artifacts.excerpt:
I went to a non-dual awareness convention:

I went to a non-dual awareness convention… but when I got there, I realized I was the convention. I was the chairs, the speakers, the awkward guy avoiding eye contact. All me. Especially the awkward guy.

The schedule was printed on a mirror. All the sessions were titled “This.” Some people brought notebooks, but the pages were blank. They said it helped.

The keynote speaker walked onstage, paused for a full minute, and said, “I am not here. You are not there.” Then he sat down. We gave him a standing ovation.

During Q&A, a guy stood up and asked, “How do I..." but stopped mid-sentence, surprised. Then he started laughing and crying at the same time. Everyone nodded. Classic first day, they said.

One breakout session was titled “How to Not Attend Anything.” It was packed. Nobody showed up. The feedback forms were all blank.

At dinner someon
